description: LFG v1.4.0 - Navigation system, Devdrive module, CCEM APM bridge fix, agent squadron, Claude Code skill, and PRD integration. Brings mobile-app-like navigation, a 4th module (devdrive), Claude Code agent/skill ecosystem, and APM telemetry to LFG.

userStories.title: Viewer navigation stack (viewer.swift)
userStories.description: As a user, I want the native viewer to maintain a navigation stack so that I can navigate between modules with back/home support.
acceptanceCriteria: viewer.swift has a navigationStack: [URL] property
navigate action handler pushes current URL to stack and loads new URL via webView.loadFileURL()
back action handler pops stack and loads previous URL (no-op if empty)
home action handler clears stack and loads splash HTML ($LFG_DIR/.lfg_splash.html)
Stack depth exposed to JS via evaluateJavaScript setting window.__lfgNavDepth
Compiles without errors: swiftc viewer.swift -framework WebKit -framework Cocoa

userStories.title: Header nav buttons (ui.js)
userStories.description: As a user, I want home and back buttons in the sticky header so that I can navigate without keyboard shortcuts.
acceptanceCriteria: Home icon (house glyph) and back arrow added before brand span in _injectHeader
Home button posts { action: 'home' } via webkit messageHandler
Back button posts { action: 'back' } via webkit messageHandler
Back button visibility toggles based on window.__lfgNavDepth > 0
Buttons use .sh-nav-btn class
No JS syntax errors when loaded in viewer

userStories.title: Header nav button styles (theme.css)
userStories.description: As a user, I want nav buttons styled consistently with the existing header aesthetic so that the UI feels cohesive.
acceptanceCriteria: .sh-nav-btn base styles added (12px icon, opacity transitions, padding)
.sh-nav-btn:hover has glow effect matching module color
.sh-nav-btn.disabled style for greyed-out back when stack empty
No CSS parse errors

userStories.title: Module scripts use navigate action
userStories.description: As a user, I want cross-module links to use in-place page transitions instead of spawning new processes.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/scan.sh command panel uses action: 'navigate' for cross-module links
lib/clean.sh command panel uses action: 'navigate' for cross-module links
lib/btau.sh command panel uses action: 'navigate' for cross-module links
lib/dashboard.sh command panel uses action: 'navigate' for cross-module links
All 4 scripts pass shellcheck or bash -n syntax check
userStories.notes: Phase 1.4 - touches scan.sh, clean.sh, btau.sh, dashboard.sh

userStories.title: Create devdrive.sh shell script
userStories.description: As a user, I want a devdrive module that bridges to yj-devdrive Python scripts so that I can manage developer drives from LFG.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/devdrive.sh created with bridges to devdrive.py, volumes.py, sparse.py
Views: volume list, symlink status, mount status, sparse image info
Command panel with: Create Devdrive, Mount, Unmount, Sync, Verify Links
LFG.init called with module: 'devdrive', context: 'Developer Drive', moduleVersion: '1.0.0'
Script passes bash -n syntax check

userStories.title: Splash screen 4th card for devdrive
userStories.description: As a user, I want a devdrive card on the splash screen so that I can launch the devdrive module from the home page.
acceptanceCriteria: .mod-dd card added with purple (#c084fc) color scheme
Card has package/drive icon, name 'DEVDRIVE', abbr 'Developer Drive'
Kbd hint shows Cmd+4
onclick calls selectModule('devdrive', this)
Keyboard handler added for metaKey && key === '4'
Script passes bash -n syntax check

userStories.title: Dashboard 4th tab for devdrive
userStories.description: As a user, I want a devdrive tab on the dashboard so that I can see volume status alongside other modules.
acceptanceCriteria: DEVDRIVE tab added to dashboard with volume status table
Tab nav responds to Cmd+4
Guidance text references 'lfg devdrive' CLI
Script passes bash -n syntax check

userStories.title: Dispatcher routing for devdrive
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run 'lfg devdrive' or 'lfg dd' from the CLI so that the devdrive module launches correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: lfg dispatcher has devdrive|dd) case routing to lib/devdrive.sh
LFG_VERSION bumped to '1.4.0'
Script passes bash -n syntax check
userStories.notes: Phase 2.4 - touches only the lfg dispatcher script

userStories.title: Menubar devdrive submenu (menubar.swift)
userStories.description: As a user, I want devdrive actions in the macOS menubar so that I can mount/unmount/sync without opening the viewer.
acceptanceCriteria: DEVDRIVE submenu added with Mount, Unmount, Sync, Status items
Methods implemented: openDevdrive(), ddMount(), ddUnmount(), ddSync()
Compiles without errors: swiftc menubar.swift -framework Cocoa

userStories.title: Fix APM bridge orchestrator endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the CCEM APM bridge orchestrator to use the correct endpoint and field names so that LFG notifications reach the APM dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: orchestrator.sh endpoint changed from POST /api/notify to POST /api/notifications/add
Field 'message' renamed to 'body'
Field 'severity' renamed to 'category'
Script passes bash -n syntax check

userStories.title: APM notification hooks in state.sh
userStories.description: As a developer, I want LFG module completions and errors to automatically notify APM so that the dashboard reflects real-time LFG activity.
acceptanceCriteria: lfg_notify_apm() function added to state.sh
Function POSTs to localhost:3031/api/notifications/add
Called from lfg_state_done and lfg_state_error
Payload includes title, body, category: 'lfg', agent_id: 'lfg-$module'
Script passes bash -n syntax check

userStories.title: Create agent spec files (scan, clean, backup)
userStories.description: As a Claude Code user, I want agent specs for scan, clean, and backup so that specialized agents can run LFG modules autonomously.
acceptanceCriteria: ~/.claude/agents/lfg/ directory created
scan-agent.md created with YAML frontmatter (name, description, tools, triggers)
clean-agent.md created with YAML frontmatter and progressive escalation docs
backup-agent.md created with YAML frontmatter and sparse image lifecycle docs
All 3 files follow the agent spec format with proper frontmatter
userStories.notes: Phase 4.1 - creates 3 new markdown files in ~/.claude/agents/lfg/

userStories.title: Create agent spec files (devdrive, monitor)
userStories.description: As a Claude Code user, I want agent specs for devdrive and monitor so that the full LFG agent squadron is available.
acceptanceCriteria: devdrive-agent.md created with YAML frontmatter and yj-devdrive bridge docs
monitor-agent.md created with YAML frontmatter and state.json watching docs
Both files follow the agent spec format with proper frontmatter
userStories.notes: Phase 4.2 - creates 2 new markdown files in ~/.claude/agents/lfg/

userStories.title: Create /lfg skill definition
userStories.description: As a Claude Code user, I want a /lfg slash command so that I can invoke LFG modules directly from Claude Code.
acceptanceCriteria: ~/.claude/commands/lfg.md created with YAML frontmatter (name, description, allowed-tools)
Subcommands defined: scan [path], clean [--force], backup [volume], devdrive [cmd], status
Each subcommand maps to ~/tools/@yj/lfg/lfg <module> <args>

userStories.title: Register /lfg in slash-commands.json
userStories.description: As a Claude Code user, I want /lfg registered in the slash command system so that it appears in command discovery.
acceptanceCriteria: /lfg entry added to ~/.claude/config/slash-commands.json
Subcommands and descriptions included
JSON is valid (passes python3 -m json.tool)

userStories.title: Register LFG triggers in reference-registry.json
userStories.description: As a Claude Code user, I want LFG-related keywords to auto-load the LFG module so that context loads intelligently.
acceptanceCriteria: LFG triggers added: lfg, disk, cache, cleanup, backup, devdrive
Points to ~/.claude/commands/lfg.md and agent specs
JSON is valid (passes python3 -m json.tool)

userStories.title: Update LFG project CLAUDE.md
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the LFG CLAUDE.md to document the agent squadron, skill commands, and devdrive module so that future sessions have full context.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent squadron section added to ~/tools/@yj/lfg/.claude/CLAUDE.md
Skill command reference section added
Devdrive module documented
File is valid markdown

userStories.title: Live integration testing and v1.4.0 tag
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all modules tested end-to-end so that v1.4.0 is a verified release.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigation flow tested: Splash -> WTFS -> Back -> DTF -> Home -> Splash
Devdrive module launches from splash and command panel works
Menubar submenus trigger correctly
APM receives LFG notifications (curl localhost:3031/api/notifications returns LFG events)
state.json updates propagate to menubar and APM
viewer.swift and menubar.swift compile without errors
All shell scripts pass bash -n syntax check
userStories.notes: Phase 8 - integration testing across all files, then git tag v1.4.0
